mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rebalance into equities.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 0.09% indicates deep accommodation regime
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with rates near zero
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% are not statistically significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above 8.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as equity valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 9.1% exceeds trigger threshold
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE > 8.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with stagnant housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand that compresses forward earnings growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1% MoM AND HOUST < 550k units
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 523k units signals below-trend housing activity
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite joint RSAFS decline and low HOUST, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 92.5 signals supply-side contraction and reduced corporate capacity utilization, which compresses profit margins and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:current INDPRO at 92.9815 is near trigger threshold
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns when INDPRO < 92.5 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals accelerating labor market deterioration that triggers a negative equity risk premium adjustment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reduce equity exposure.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 100k MoM AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 131043k and UNRATE at 9.1% indicate labor stress
regime_hint: labor market shock or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between PAYEMS change and UNRATE change is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured at quarterly frequency serves as a leading indicator of corporate earnings trajectory; sustained GDP growth below 1.5% annualized signals weak demand and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as growth expectations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13441.9B as of t-5mo provides lagged demand signal
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in low-growth quarters are not significantly lower than high-growth quarters (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI (CPIAUCSL) rising above 3% combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2%) signals monetary tightening that increases equity discount rates and reduces forward valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 224.804 and FEDFUNDS at 0.09% currently indicate low inflation-rate environment
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation concerns
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite joint inflation and rate elevation, or the interaction coefficient between CPI and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) serve as a leading indicator of construction activity and consumer confidence; a sustained level above 600k units signals robust demand and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors increase equity allocations in response to demand strength.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 523k units is below trigger threshold
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns when HOUST > 600k are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
